title: ON CONFLICT on a PK column in a WITHOUT ROWID table does not work
description:
drh added on 2021-08-18 22:16:34:
In a WITHOUT ROWID table, if there is an ON CONFLICT clause on a NOT NULL
constraint of a PRIMARY KEY column, then that ON CONFLICT clause is ignored
and the conflict is always resolved as ABORT.  For example:

CREATE TABLE t1(
  a INT NOT NULL ON CONFLICT REPLACE DEFAULT 3,
  b TEXT,
  c TEXT,
  PRIMARY KEY(a,b)
) /*WITHOUT ROWID*/
INSERT INTO t1(a,b,c) VALUES
  (1,'two','three'),
  (NULL,'four','five'),
  (6,'seven','eight');
SELECT * FROM t1;

The code above inserts three rows into the table, with "a" values of 1, 3, and 6.
The 3 value arises because the "NOT NULL ON CONFLICT REPLACE" causes the NULL to
be replaced by the default value of 3.  But if you uncomment the "/WITHOUT ROWID/
it does not work → the INSERT statement fails with a NOT NULL constraint error
and no rows are inserted.
This problem has apparently always existed in the code, going back to the introduction
of WITHOUT ROWID in version 3.8.2 (2013-12-06).  The problem was discovered by code
inspection and has not been observed in the wild.
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE t2(a INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 5, b TEXT, c REAL, PRIMARY KEY(a, b));
INSERT INTO t2(a, b, c) VALUES (2, 'one', 1.1), (NULL, 'two', 2.2), (3, 'three', 3.3);
INSERT INTO t2(a, b, c) VALUES (2, 'one', 1.1), (5, 'four', 4.4);
CREATE TABLE t3(x TEXT, y INT, z TEXT DEFAULT 'default', PRIMARY KEY(x, y));
key_bug_features: ON CONFLICT clause ignored in WITHOUT ROWID table
